banner.title: The "schema (vocabulary)" does not disappear — it is just <1>shared</1>
banner.sub: This is <1>vocabulary automatically introspected from the actual data</1> (across all datasets; labels come from the TBox projected from each dataset's schema). The more it is aligned, the better cross-dataset search and comparison work.

schemaError: Could not fetch the vocabulary of the actual data (the query layer is not responding).

classesCard.title: Shared classes (actual data)
classesCard.note1: The number on the right = count in the actual data. <1>Classes are instance counts</1> (how many things of that type exist), <3>predicates are usage counts</3> (number of triples using that property).
classesCard.note2: * Only <1>canonical (published, citable) data</1> is counted. Draft (unreviewed) graphs are not included — they enter the tally once published.
classesCard.reusesSubhead: Reused vocabulary (detected from the actual data namespaces)

usersCard.title: Datasets using this vocabulary
usersCard.empty: No ingested datasets yet. Once you save a design in the workbench and ingest it, it will appear here as a cross-dataset target.

caution.title: Why "handle with care"?
caution.body: Rewriting a shared class ripples into the search and answers of every dataset that uses it. Confirm changes after reviewing the <1>impact preview</1>.

known.qudt: QUDT (shared vocabulary for quantities and units)
known.cmso: CMSO (computational material samples — crystal structure, composition, defects)
